meta.title: Computer Engineering Complete Curriculum
meta.description: All domains, subjects, chapters, sub-topics and concepts for a Computer Engineering degree and beyond

concepts.name: use in modifying pointer in function
memory layout of a C program
open addressing (linear probing, quadratic probing, double hashing)
O(n log n) lower bound proof
addressing modes (immediate, direct, indirect, register, indexed, relative)
page replacement algorithms (FIFO, LRU, Optimal, Clock)
specification → RTL → synthesis → floorplanning → placement → routing → DRC/LVS → tapeout
system call types (process, file, device, info, communication)
process states (new, ready, running, waiting, terminated)
scheduling criteria (CPU utilization, throughput, turnaround, waiting, response time)
conditions for deadlock (mutual exclusion, hold and wait, no preemption, circular wait)
ls, cd, pwd, find, locate, du, df
grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, uniq, wc, tr
ps, top, htop, kill, nice, nohup, jobs, fg, bg
ip, ifconfig, netstat, ss, ping, traceroute, curl, wget, nmap, tcpdump, iptables
Type 1 (bare-metal): VMware ESXi, Microsoft Hyper-V, Xen, KVM
namespaces (PID, net, mnt, uts, ipc, user)
congestion control (slow start, AIMD, CUBIC, BBR)
DNS record types (A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT, NS, SOA, PTR)
control plane vs data plane separation
constraints (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, CHECK, DEFAULT)
joins (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL, CROSS, SELF)
window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG, LEAD, SUM OVER, PARTITION BY)
join algorithms (nested loop, sort-merge, hash join)
Redis data structures (string, hash, list, set, sorted set, stream)
use cases (social networks, recommendation engines, fraud detection)
phases (requirement, design, implementation, testing, deployment, maintenance)
common NP-complete problems (3-SAT, vertex cover, clique, subset sum, traveling salesman)
hooks (useState, useEffect, useContext, useReducer, useMemo, useCallback, useRef)
probability distributions (Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Normal, Exponential, Uniform)
Type I and Type II errors
metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, MSE, RMSE, MAE, R²)
activation functions (ReLU, sigmoid, tanh, GELU, Swish, Leaky ReLU)
learning rate scheduling (step decay, cosine annealing, warmup)
LSTM gates (input, forget, output, cell state)
PEFT (LoRA, QLoRA, prefix tuning, prompt tuning, adapter tuning)
vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, Qdrant, pgvector)
block cipher modes (ECB, CBC, CTR, GCM, CCM)
key derivation functions (PBKDF2, bcrypt, Argon2, scrypt)
CTF categories (pwn, web, crypto, forensics, reverse engineering, misc)
S3 (buckets, storage classes, lifecycle, presigned URLs)
VPC (subnets, route tables, IGW, NAT gateway, security groups, NACLs)
algorithms (round robin, least connections, IP hash, weighted)
session guarantees (read your writes, monotonic reads)
SPI (CPOL, CPHA, CS, MOSI, MISO, CLK)
threat to RSA/ECC from Shor's algorithm
DAST tools (OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite CI)
secrets management in pipelines (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
